issues.comment: The formula `Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1` is wrong when `user_count` is evenly divisible by `limit`. For example: 50 users, limit 50 → `Math.floor(1) + 1 = 2` (should be 1); 100 users, limit 50 → `Math.floor(2) + 1 = 3` (should be 2). This causes a phantom empty page to always be shown at the end when counts are exact multiples of limit. Since `showingLast` compares `currentPage` to `totalPages`, the "next" button stays enabled and navigates to an empty results page. The correct formula is `Math.ceil(user_count / limit)`.

`group.users.delete(user_id)` is used instead of `group.remove(user)`. The `Group#remove` method is the standard method used throughout the codebase which destroys the `group_user` record and also runs `publish_category_updates`, `trigger_user_removed_event`, and `enqueue_user_removed_from_group_webhook_events`. Using the low-level `group.users.delete(user_id)` only removes the association record, bypassing all those important side effects.

The test uses `xhr :put, :remove_member` but the route declares `delete "members" => "groups#remove_member"`. While this still passes in controller specs (which invoke actions directly without routing), it means the test doesn't verify the correct HTTP method contract. The other `remove_member` test in the same file correctly uses `xhr :delete`.
